raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section opens with an explicit 'central contribution' claim for SG-Constraint and closes with a 'principal methodological contribution' claim for tube-MPC dominance; both require scrutiny for clarity, coherence, and whether the contribution delta is accurately scoped.","R1.2":"The argument chain runs from benchmark design properties → numerical results → a broad conclusion about robust-control superiority over safe-RL; that inferential chain must be checked for premises (is the scoring rubric neutral?), intermediates (do tasks 7–10 cherry-pick a disturbance regime that structurally favors tube-MPC?), and the concluding generalization.","R1.3":"Concrete scores (0.81±0.03, 0.62±0.08, 0.47±0.11) and 'one to two orders of magnitude' violation-rate reductions are load-bearing quantitative claims; alignment with any accompanying tables, figures, or appendices must be verified.","R1.4":"Novelty is asserted on two independent fronts—the benchmark suite as a new standardized artifact and the tube-MPC dominance finding as a new empirical result—both require assessment against Park and Wei (2024) and the broader safe-RL/robust-control benchmarking literature."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is this a technical/content review (evaluating logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty of contributions) or a prose/writing review (improving sentence quality, paragraph flow, and clarity)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with an explicit 'central contribution' claim for SG-Constraint and closes with a 'principal methodological contribution' claim for tube-MPC dominance; both require scrutiny for clarity, coherence, and whether the contribution delta is accurately scoped.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain runs from benchmark design properties → numerical results → a broad conclusion about robust-control superiority over safe-RL; that inferential chain must be checked for premises (is the scoring rubric neutral?), intermediates (do tasks 7–10 cherry-pick a disturbance regime that structurally favors tube-MPC?), and the concluding generalization.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete scores (0.81±0.03, 0.62±0.08, 0.47±0.11) and 'one to two orders of magnitude' violation-rate reductions are load-bearing quantitative claims; alignment with any accompanying tables, figures, or appendices must be verified.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty is asserted on two independent fronts—the benchmark suite as a new standardized artifact and the tube-MPC dominance finding as a new empirical result—both require assessment against Park and Wei (2024) and the broader safe-RL/robust-control benchmarking literature.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this a technical/content review (evaluating logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty of contributions) or a prose/writing review (improving sentence quality, paragraph flow, and clarity)?
